sutta: now brahmin dhona visited the exalted 1 and greeted him and after exchanging the customary words of greeting sat down at 1 side so seated brahman donna said to the exalted 1 i have heard it said master gotama that master gotama does not salute aged venerable brahmins well stricken in years long on life's road grown old nor rise up for them nor offer them a seat master gautama it is just so master gautama does none of these things to aged memorable brahmins this is not right master gautama and the buddha said do you not profess to be a brahmin donor and dhona said if of anyone master gotama in speaking rightly it should be said the brahmin is well born on both sides pure in descent as far back as 7 generations both of mother and father unchallenged and without reproach in point of birth studius carrying the mantras in mind a past master in the 3 vedas with the indices and ritual in phonology too and fifthly in the legends an expert in verse and grammar skilled in reading the marks of a great man in speculation on the universe to be sure of me master gautama in speaking rightly that thing should be said for i must go to ma i'm so born i'm so skilled i'll just stop here for a while so here this brahmin is criticizing the buddha for not paying respect to all aged brahmins and then instead of answering him directly the buddha asked him a question are you brahmin and then he said that he spoke about the 3 marks of a brahmin the first 1 is that a brahmin appear brahmina is pure on the for 7 generations he's born of pure uh blood brahmins on the mother and father's side for 7 generations that's the first mark of a brahmin the second 1 is that he's learned in the vedas in the mantras etc the third 1 is he has worldly knowledge that means he is killed in the marks of a great man in speculation about the universe etc so he said he has all these 3 marks of a brahmin and the buddha said dhona those brahmin sages of all mantra makers mantra says whose ancient collection of mantra verses hymns and sayings brahmins now ever him ever say ever word the word ever have the saying said namely these 5 brahmins have declared the brahma line the bounded the breaker of bounds and fifthly the brahmin outcast which of them donor are you and dhona said we know not of these 5 brahmins master gotama yet we know that we are brahmins it were well for me if master gautama would teach me dhamma so that i may know of these 5 i stopped here for a while here the buddha is saying that those brahmana sages of all and he named them etc you see the buddha he knows the he has this ability to see the past so because of his ability and his psychic power he could name all these brahmin sages of all and he says these are mantra makers mantra is a kind of words which have no meaning they are supposed to be derived from the spiritual world but they have a certain power behind them so these brahmins they keep these mantras very secret and they will never divulge these secrets to other cars they only keep it among their cars and they use this mantras quite often and sometimes uh when they view these mantras and they have certain power that's why other cars sometimes are afraid of them so the buddha is saying that these uh brahmin sages of all they have declared there are 5 types of brahmins like the brahma like the deva like the bounded the breaker of bounds and the brahmin outcast and he asked dhona which category do you fall under and donna say he never heard of these 5 brahmins so the here he's asking the buddha to explain these 5 classes of brahmins to him then the buddha said then listen brahman give heed and i will speak yes sir replied he and the exalted 1 said and how dhona becomes a brahmin brahma like take the case donor of a brahmin who is well born on both sides pure and descent as far back as 7 generations both of mother and father unchallenged and without reproach in point of birth he for 8 and 40 years leads the brahma life of virginity applying himself to the mantras then completing that course he seeks the teacher's fee for teaching according to dhamma not non-dharma and what there is dharma donor never as plow man nor trader nor cowherd nor bowman nor raja's man nor buy any craft to get this living but solely by going about for arms despising not the beggar's bowl and he hands over the teacher's fee for teaching and has his hair and beard shaped off dawns the yellow rope and goes forth from the home to the homeless life and thus gone forth he abides in mind pervading with goodwill 1 quarter of the world so a second a third and a fourth quarter then above below across and everywhere the whole wide world he pervades with thoughts of goodwill far-reaching expansive measureless without hatred or ill will she abides in mind pervading with compassion with joy with equanimity 1 quarter of the world so a second a third and a fourth quarter then above below across everywhere the whole wide world he pervades with thoughts of compassion joy and equanimity far-reaching expansive measureless without hatred or ill will and having made these 4 brahma abidings become on the breaking up of the body after death he arises in the well-faring brahma world thus dhona a brahmin becomes brahmaline and how donna becomes a brahmin diva take the case donor of a brahman of similar birth and conduct he does not get a living by plowing and so forth but by going about for arms he hands over the teacher's fee for teaching and seeks a wife according to dhamma not the non-dharma and what there is dhamma not with 1 bod or soul but only with a brahmani on whom water has been poured and he goes only to a brahmani not to the daughter of a noble or the merchant class or the laborer cast nor to the daughter of an outcast a hunter a bamboo worker a card maker or aboriginal nor go see to a woman with child nor to 1 giving some nor to 1 not in her season and why the owner goes not a brahmin to 1 with child if he go the boy or girl will surely be foully born therefore he goes not and why goes he not to 1 giving son if he go the boy or girl will surely be an unclean suckling therefore he goes not and wherefore on goes he not to 1 not in her season if donna a brahmin go to 1 in her season never for him does the brahmani become a means for lust for sport for pleasure the brahmani is for the brahmin just a means to be good of spring and the buddha continued and when in red long he has begotten a child he has his hair and beard shaved off and goes forth and being thus gone forth aloof from sensual desires he enters and abides in the first the second the third and the fourth janus and having made these 4 janas become on the breaking up of the body after death he arises in the well-faring heaven world thus dona a brahmin becomes deva like and how donna becomes a brahmin bounded take the case donor of a brahmin of similar similar birth and conduct who wears in like manner and when in wedlock he has begotten a child the fondness for children obsesses him and he settles on the family estate and does not go forth on the home to the homeless life in the bounds of the brahmins of all he stays and transgres and transgresses them not and it is said within bounds he keeps and transgresses not and therefore the brahmin is called bounded thus dhona the brahmin becomes bounded and how donna becomes a brahmin a breaker of bounds take the case donor of a brahmana of similar birth and conduct he hands over the teacher's fee and seeks a wife either according to dhamma or non-dharma 1 bod or soul or a brahmani on whom the water pouring ceremony has been performed he goes to a brahmani or to the daughter of a noble or of a merchant caste man or of a laborer cast to the daughter of an outcast or a hunter or a bamboo worker or a card maker or an aboriginal he goes to a woman with child to 1 giving suck to 1 in her season to 1 not in her season and for him the brahmani becomes just a means for lust for sport and for pleasure or to begin offspring and he keeps not within the ancient brahmin bounds but transgresses them and it is said he keeps not within bounds but transgresses and therefore he is called a breaker of bounds thus donna the brahmin becomes a breaker of bounds and how donna becomes a brahmin a brahmana outcast take the case donor of a brahmin of similar birth he for 8 and 40 years leads the brahma life of virginity applying himself to the mantras then completing that course he seeks the teacher's fee for teaching he gets his living according to dhamma or non dhamma as a plow man a trader a cowherd a bowman a rajas man or by some craft or despising not the beggar's bowl just by going about for arms on handing back the teacher's fee he seeks a wife according to dhamma or non dhamma 1 bird or soul or a brahmani on whom water has been born he goes to a brahmani or any other woman 1 with child giving suck and so forth and she is for him a means for lust or to begin offspring he leads a life doing all these things then the brahmana said us of him how is it that an honorable brahman leads this sort of life and to this he replies just as fire burns clean things or unclean and not by that is the fire defile even so good sirs if a brahma if a brahman lead a life doing all these things not by doing that is a brahmin defile and it is said he leads a life doing all these things and therefore he is called a brahmin outcast thus donna a brahmin becomes a brahmin outcast verily donna those brahmin sages of all mantra makers mantra says whose ancient collection of mantra verses hymns and sayings brahmins now still him say word each word and have the saying said namely ataka and the rest these 5 brahmans declare have declared the brahma like the deva like the bounded the breaker of bounds and fifthly the brahmin outcast which of them donor are you and dhona said if such there are master we at least do not fulfill the ways of the brahmin outcast but it is marvelous what you say master gotama let master gotama take me as a lay disciple gone to his refuge henceforth as life as long as life laughs that's the end of the sutta
commentary:
this is 1 of the sutras where you can see the buddha with his vast knowledge he can teach a brahmin even the brahmin tradition that they don't know of and here is describing the 5 types of brahmins now you notice all of them for the first 48 years of their life they lead a life of virginity and then they learn the mantras and after that they start teaching and then if they follow their tradition then they are supposed to only beg for their food not to take up a trade and the first 3 types are still acceptable to them that means uh the first 1 after 48 years of learning then he renounces becomes a renuncian and practices the brahma abiding brahmaviharas which brings him to rebirth in the brahma world the second 1 he seeks a wife but only a brahmana on whom water has been been poured that means i suppose means that this has been given to him because the ceremony of pouring water i think implies that giving so he doesn't go to any other woman of any other cast only to a brahmana and once he has a child with this brahmani then he shaves off his hair and goes forth and attains the janus so that after that he's reborn in the heaven world there's a second class the third 1 after 48 years of learning then he gets married also also to brahmin brahmani and after that after he has a child instead of renouncing he is attached to the child and he stays in the home he doesn't renounce still that is acceptable but the last 2 are not acceptable because um the the last 2 1 they might marry a brahmani or they might not marry a brahmani once they marry out of their cast that is not acceptable and also because of the other conditions mentioned so this um suta is interesting from because it gives us an insight into the life of a brahmin during those times because nowadays you hardly see this happen and nowadays you don't see brahmins who are virgins for 48 years of their life learning all the mantras they have kind of lost their tradition
